_core_paths_doc: BLUE crates per CLAUDE.md. ade_runtime is RED (shell); ade_testkit and ade_node are neither. ade_network has per-submodule BLUE scoping: mux::frame is BLUE; mux::transport and session are RED. ade_core::consensus (S-B1 onward) is BLUE under the already-listed crate prefix. Substring matches against absolute file paths.
_replay_cmd_doc: ade_testkit hosts boundary_replay, differential_*_replay, ledger_determinism, and stateful_replay test suites.
_replay_on_stop_doc: Disabled by default. Phase 4 test runtime is acceptable but full replay across all eras is not sub-second; flip to true once selective replay is introduced.
_clusters_dir_doc: Canonical IDD location. Currently contains PHASE4-N-D (in-flight). Closed Phase 0A/0B/1/2A/2C clusters remain at docs/completed/phase_*/ as historical anchors.
_cluster_id_format_doc: Cluster IDs use the PHASE<N>-<LETTER> convention (e.g., PHASE4-N-A, PHASE4-N-D). Slash commands pass the cluster id as the literal directory name under docs/clusters/.
_clusters_archive_dir_doc: Where /cluster-close moves cluster docs upon closure. Created on demand.
_planning_doc_doc: Active phase plan. /cluster-doc reads this when seeding new cluster docs (N-A, N-B, N-C, N-E, N-F).
_grounding_docs_dir_doc: Where the four grounding docs live: ade-CODEMAP.md, ade-SEAMS.md, ade-HEAD_DELTAS.md, ade-TRACEABILITY.md.
_head_deltas_baseline_doc: PHASE4-N-F-G-R close + C1-evidence baseline (HEAD `65954fa3`, 2026-06-04): set by the full grounding-doc CATCH-UP refresh on 2026-06-05, which narrated the `550eec3a..65954fa3` span -- the G-J close-pass + clusters G-K..G-R + the C1 genesis-rehearsal evidence -- bringing all four grounding docs current from the G-J pin (`550eec3a`) to 11 crates / 458 canonical types / 134 CI checks / 328 registry rules (+9 rules G-K..G-R all enforced; +8 CI gates). The next cluster's /head-deltas measures from `65954fa3`; bump on each cluster close to keep HEAD_DELTAS per-cluster and reviewable. NOTE: the previously-recorded value `853344f7` was stale and MISLABELED (it is PHASE4-N-F-G-L S2, not 'PHASE4-N-F-G-K close'); the catch-up used the correct `550eec3a` -- the last HEAD the docs reflected -- which captured the otherwise-skipped G-K (DC-NODE-09) and the G-J close-pass. Prior per-cluster baseline history follows below, and in git history of this file + ade-HEAD_DELTAS.md. PRIOR baseline: PHASE4-N-F-G-J close baseline (HEAD `550eec3a`, PHASE4-N-F-G-J S5, 2026-06-03; genesis-successor block correctness — block 0 PrevHash::Genesis/null wire authority + position rule + cold-start reachability + C1 rehearsal harness; narrated in ade-HEAD_DELTAS.md §0; the next cluster's /head-deltas measures from here). PRIOR baseline: PHASE4-N-F-G-D close (HEAD `6bd60c80`, PHASE4-N-F-G-D S4, 2026-06-02). HEAD_DELTAS for the N-F-G-D close narrates the `6f848825 -> 6bd60c80` span: the G-E close tail `da205bff` + the PHASE4-N-F-G-D bounty dry-run cluster (S1 path-fidelity proof+fence ci_check_node_path_fidelity.sh; S2 NEW GREEN ade_node::rehearsal_evidence PrivateRehearsalManifest + RED ade_node::rehearsal_pass + ci_check_rehearsal_manifest_schema.sh; S3 C1 dry-run runbook + env-gated operator harness node_c1_dry_run_rehearsal; S4 close-surfaced security fix — rehearsal leak gate scans active+archived bounty homes fail-closed + regression test rehearsal_gate_archived_home). CN-REHEARSAL-FIDELITY-01 NEW + enforced (314->315); 119->121 CI (+ci_check_node_path_fidelity.sh + ci_check_rehearsal_manifest_schema.sh); 456 canonical types unchanged (NO BLUE change). NARROW: the rehearsal HARNESS is enforced (path-faithful + non-promotable); NO RO-LIVE flip, NO bounty/preview/preprod completion claim — the live C1 execution stays blocked_until_operator_c1_net_executed. This baseline (`6bd60c80`) is the N-F-G-D slice-span HEAD; the next cluster's /head-deltas measures from here. --- HISTORICAL (prior N-F-G-E baseline 6f848825, PHASE4-N-F-G-E S1, 2026-06-02). HEAD_DELTAS for the N-F-G-E close narrated the `90791691 -> 6f848825` span: the G-C close tail `351d46bc` + the G-E cluster (one slice — GREEN session::core 16 MiB reassembly-tail cap MAX_REASSEMBLY_TAIL_BYTES + additive SessionError::ReassemblyBufferOverflow drop-the-peer; RED node_sync 256-block WirePump lookahead cap MAX_WIRE_PUMP_LOOKAHEAD; new gate ci_check_live_feed_memory_bounds.sh; DC-LIVEMEM-01 enforced, 313->314). 118->119 CI; 456 canonical types unchanged (NO BLUE change). NARROW claim: peer-driven memory bounded BEFORE authoritative decode/apply -- NOT full network DoS resistance, NOT peer fairness, NOT BA-02/live-evidence readiness. This baseline (`6f848825`) is the N-F-G-E slice-span HEAD; the next cluster's /head-deltas measures from here. --- HISTORICAL (prior N-F-G-C baseline 90791691, PHASE4-N-F-G-C S2, 2026-06-02). HEAD_DELTAS for the N-F-G-C close narrated the `339cccb1 -> 90791691` span: the G-B close tail `febee120` + the G-C cluster (S1 live NodeBlockSource::WirePump feed on the --mode node On arm via the reused closed dial_for_admission+run_admission_wire_pump; S2 NEW RED ade_node::ba02_pass BA-02 evidence wiring over the GREEN correlate). 117->118 CI (+ci_check_ba02_evidence_manifest_schema.sh; ci_check_served_chain_handoff_fence.sh broadened in place); 313 rules unchanged; 456 canonical types unchanged (NO BLUE change). RO-LIVE-01 stays partial / RO-LIVE-06 stays enforced -- G-C closes the MECHANICAL live-feed + evidence scaffolding ONLY; the operator-witnessed live ACCEPT is still owed (blocked_until_operator_stake_available). This baseline (`90791691`) is the N-F-G-C slice-span HEAD; the next cluster's /head-deltas measures from here. --- HISTORICAL (prior N-F-G-B baseline 339cccb1, PHASE4-N-F-G-B S3 gate fix, 2026-06-01). HEAD_DELTAS for the N-F-G-B close narrated the `80dac1f7 -> 339cccb1` span (the G-A close tail -- `62cb8718` G-A close + `1806584c` gate-binding follow-up -- plus the PHASE4-N-F-G-B self-accept->serve handoff cluster on the `--mode node` spine: S1 GREEN `SelfAcceptedHandoff` carrier (newtype over the BLUE `AcceptedBlock`, private field, sole ctor `from_self_accepted`) + `run_real_forge` wrapper-split surfacing the original self-accepted token via a sibling return component (NO new `CoordinatorEvent` variant); S2 dispatcher-spawned sibling served-chain admit task draining a typed `mpsc<SelfAcceptedHandoff>` to the single `ServedChainHandle::push_atomic` fed only by `into_accepted()` (relay-loop body forwards a typed channel send only; containment gate byte-unchanged); S3 hermetic loopback proving the served block-fetch payload tag-24-unwraps to the self-accept input bytes + new additive gate `ci_check_served_chain_handoff_fence.sh`; 10 commits / +3391 / -1600). DC-NODE-06 flipped declared->enforced at close (+ 2 strengthenings: CN-PROD-04, CN-CONS-07); 1 new CI gate (116->117). NO BLUE crate changed (456 canonical types unchanged); 313 registry rules (count unchanged); 1 new GREEN module (`ade_runtime::producer::self_accepted_handoff`). No real socket/peer wired -- hermetic loopback only; live serve + operator-peer ACCEPT = G-C (RO-LIVE-01/06 stay partial). This baseline (`339cccb1`) is the N-F-G-B slice-span HEAD; the next cluster's /head-deltas measures its span from here. Bump on each cluster close to keep HEAD_DELTAS per-cluster and reviewable.
_normative_docs_doc: Files /traceability reads to extract rules. Tier-gate statement and its Tier 5 addendum carry the most enforced rules; the contract docs anchor cross-surface invariants; CLAUDE.md carries the commit-attribution policy.
_canonical_type_registry_doc: No separate canonical-type registry file yet; canonical-type rules live inline in the invariant registry under family T.
_invariant_registry_doc: Canonical, append-only invariant registry. Schema follows ziranity-v3's constitution_rule_registry.toml (base fields identical), additively compatible with the IDD template. 328 entries at HEAD (196 enforced / 20 partial / 112 declared / 0 deprecated) across families T (true), CN (constraint network), DC (derived constraint), OP (operational), RO (release obligation). Recent additions through PHASE4-N-F-G-R (all enforced): DC-NODE-09 (G-K, serve-listener lifetime), CN-WIRE-10 (G-L, serve-side N2N handshake V15 cardano-node compat -- live-confirmed), CN-WIRE-11 (G-M, real-node ChainSync FindIntersect + ArrayHead), T-REC-04 + DC-CINPUT-03 (G-N, WarmStart eta0 seed-epoch sidecar), CN-WIRE-12 (G-O, feed-side BlockFetch tag-24 unwrap), DC-CINPUT-04 (G-P, feed header-validation view), DC-NODE-10 (G-Q, forge-successor evolved-spine position), DC-NODE-11 (G-R, monotone served-chain stability gate).
_ci_dirs_doc: CI scripts live as `ci/ci_check_*.sh` (ci_dirs = ["ci"]). A non-gating .github/workflows/notify-atlas.yml dispatch workflow also exists (grounding-doc -> ade-atlas rebuild trigger), but it is not an Ade invariant gate; add '.github/workflows' to ci_dirs only if/when an actual invariant gate is added there.
